_comment: The temporal spine of the Mended Mirror. Skin-agnostic data: the Scroll and the Astrolabe both read this. Years are integers, negative = BCE, positive = CE. 'to' = 2026 means living-to-present. certainty: attested | inferred | reconstructed | contested | deep (deep-time continuous, datable only as 'very old') | prophesied (future). Axis note for skins: the span runs ~-50000 to 2026, so the time axis MUST be non-linear (compress deep past, expand the last ~5000 years) or everything piles on the right edge.

highlights.name: The Axial Age
highlights.note: Within a few centuries: the Buddha, Mahavira, the Hebrew prophets, the Greek philosophers, Laozi & Confucius — a planetary simultaneity of awakening. The scroll should make this band glow.

aboriginal.note: The Dreaming — among the oldest continuous living cultures on Earth (~50,000+ yrs).

san.note: The |Xam/San — deep-time continuous; rock-art tens of millennia old.

pie.note: Proto-Indo-European homeland & dispersal; a linguistic reconstruction, not an attestation.

mesopotamian.note: Sumer → Babylon; last datable cuneiform (Uruk almanac) 79/80 CE.

egyptian.note: Dynastic; last hieroglyphs ~394 CE, Philae closed 537.

basque.note: Pre-Indo-European substrate; recoverable mostly through late folklore.

sami.note: Indigenous Fennoscandia; the deep date is inferred from Iron-Age archaeology (direct records only 17th–18th c.); living, with active revival.

canaanite.note: Ugarit/Levant; continued as Phoenician-Punic to the fall of Carthage (146 BCE).

hittite.note: Anatolian Bronze Age; ended at the Bronze Age collapse.

vedic.note: Rigvedic; continues as living Hindu tradition (Vedic→Hindu).

dravidian.note: Pre/para-Vedic South Indian substrate; living Tamil Shaivism & folk cults.

zoroastrian.note: Zoroaster's date debated (linguistic ~1200–1000 BCE vs traditional ~6th c. BCE); living among Parsis/Iranis.

abrahamic.note: Composite: Israelite Yahwism → Judaism / Christianity / Islam. Deserves its own splitting/deepening pass.

chinese.note: Shang → the I Ching/Laozi/Confucius axial flowering → living Taoism & folk religion.

mesoamerican.note: Composite: Olmec → Maya → Aztec; ended at the Conquest, with folk continuity.

finnish.note: Finnic pagan oral substrate; Christianized ~1200; compiled as the Kalevala in 1835.

andean.note: Chavín → Inca; ended at the Conquest, with Quechua/Aymara folk continuity.

tengrist.note: Steppe sky-cult; Tengri first attested ~4th c. BCE (Xiongnu) → Göktürk peak → living revival; substrate plausibly older.

arabian.note: Pre-Islamic Arabian; the Kaaba idols destroyed 630 CE with the rise of Islam.

korean.note: Foundation myth dates Dangun to 2333 BCE; muism/folk religion is living.

slavic.note: Rooted earlier; attested late; Christianization of Rus 988.

baltic.note: Conservative IE paganism; Lithuania was Europe's last pagan state (1387); revived as Romuva.

ainu.note: Jōmon-rooted; Ainu culture proper from ~13th c.; living, with revival.

tupi.note: Tupi-Guaraní; pre-contact, much recorded post-contact; some Guaraní communities continue.

pueblo.note: Ancestral Puebloan from Basketmaker II horticultural roots (maize ~1500 BCE→); Chaco/Mesa Verde peak ~1100; Hopi & Zuni continuous.

khoekhoe.note: Khoe pastoralists ~2000 yrs in southern Africa; the traditional religion was largely displaced by Christianity by ~1900, but the Nama survive with language, oral tradition & cultural revival.

gnostic.note: Gnostic/Hermetic/Manichaean; Manichaeism survived among the Uyghurs & in SE China into the 14th c.

taino.note: Greater Antilles Arawak; devastated after 1492; revival movements today.

polynesian.note: Proto-Polynesian pantheon crystallized in the Tonga–Samoa homeland (~1000–500 BCE, Lapita); East-Polynesian/Māori florescence ~1250–1500; living Māori tradition.

yoruba.note: Ife florescence; living, with vast diaspora (Santería, Candomblé).

dogon.note: Living; the Sirius/Nommo cosmology rests on Griaule's contested ethnography.

kongo.note: Kongo kingdom roots; living, with diaspora.

inuit.note: Thule culture ~1000 CE → Inuit (the figures are Thule-Inuit); rests on older Paleo-Eskimo (Pre-Dorset/Dorset) Arctic roots; living.

haudenosaunee.note: Confederacy founding contested: Haudenosaunee oral tradition (eclipse-dated) ~1142 vs mainstream archaeology ~1450; living.

navajo.note: Diné migration to the Southwest ~1400; living.

buddhist.note: From the Buddha — death-date contested (long chronology ~483 BCE vs short ~400 BCE); living across Asia & beyond.

bon.note: Archaic Tibetan substrate (undatable) systematized as Yungdrung Bön ~11th c. CE alongside Buddhism; living.

castaneda.note: The forgery — precisely datable to its publication run (1968–98).

christ.note: The historical Jesus ~4 BCE–30 CE; the risen Christ as worshipped develops after.

castaneda-tonal-nagual.note: The fabricated shard, datable to publication.

ein-sof.note: The Kabbalistic Ein Sof crystallizes ~12th–13th c. (Sefer ha-Zohar).

saoshyant.note: The Zoroastrian end-time savior — a future, not a past; lives to the RIGHT of now.

frashokereti.note: The final renovation of the world — eschatological future.

tupi-yvymarane.note: The Land-Without-Evil — a paradise still sought; a future on the scroll.

indra|perun.note: Divergence: both inherit the PIE storm-god *Perkʷūnos.

baal|yahweh.note: Israelite adoption of Canaanite storm/Chaoskampf imagery (Iron Age).

demeter|isis.note: Hellenistic syncretism of the two great mother-goddesses.

christ|osiris.note: The dying-rising parallel — real but Frazer over-read it; held at arm's length, not asserted.

sami-horagalles|thor.note: Norse loan laid over the indigenous thunder-god during the Viking age.

tupi-tupa|yahweh.note: Jesuit elevation of Tupã to 'God' in the reductions.

hawaiian-kane|tane.note: Divergence: Hawaiian Kāne and Māori Tāne split as Polynesians settled the far islands.

tonal-nagual|yin-yang.note: Horden’s The Toltec I Ching (2009) — a documented modern synthesis.

castaneda-tonal-nagual|tonal-nagual.note: The forgery published — its seam points home to the real line it imitated.
